html:
[1008]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 自睦州通判替归京师 - Formal expression indicating the end of an official term and return to the capital.
  • 檥舟宿泗间 - Moored the boat and stayed overnight in Si Prefecture.
  • 目莹唇朱 - Descriptive phrase highlighting the physical appearance of the beggar.
  • 吾卖豆每粒千二百钱 - Straightforward statement about the beggar's occupation and pricing.
  • 吾适乏钱 - Simple statement of lack of money.
  • 只有所衣绵袄以奉偿如何 - Question about the value of the coat as a form of payment.
  • 固可也 - Brief affirmation.
  • 容取豆 - Request for time to get the beans.
  • 以纸一幅于两乳间擦摩之 - Method of producing beans by rubbing a piece of paper between the two breasts.
  • 辄有黑豆数粒出 - Result of the rubbing, with several black beans appearing.
  • 取一与刘 - Action of giving one bean to Liu.
  • 掷其余汴水中 - Disposal of the remaining beans by throwing them into the Bian River.
  • 即日食如初而益多 - Describes the improvement in Liu's appetite after swallowing the beans.
  • 后面色如丹 - Describes a physical change in Liu's appearance, with his face turning red like cinnabar.
  • 但每岁一发 - Indicates the periodic nature of Liu's illness, recurring once a year.
  • 渴必饮水数斗 - Describes Liu's excessive thirst when ill.
  • 觉二豆在腹中如枣大 - Describes Liu's internal sensation of feeling two beans in his abdomen as large as dates.
  • 乞人又约某年相见于淮西 - Indicates a future meeting with the beggar in Huai West.
  • 不知如何也 - Expresses the translator's uncertainty about the beggar's words.
  • 右二事见浮休集 - Indicates the source of the stories, recorded in the *Fuxiu Ji*.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 睦州 - A prefecture in the southeastern part of present-day Zhejiang Province.
  • 通判 - An official position in the Song Dynasty, responsible for overseeing local administration and finances.
  • 京师 - The capital city, which was Bianjing (present-day Kaifeng) during the Song Dynasty.
  • 泗间 - Si Prefecture, a place along the Bian River.
  • 浮休集 - A collection of stories or anecdotes, likely a literary work from the Song Dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 刘士彦 - Liu Shiyi, the translator of the text.
  • 睦州 - Muzhou, a prefecture in the southeastern part of present-day Zhejiang Province.
  • 京师 - Bianjing (present-day Kaifeng), the capital city during the Song Dynasty.
  • 泗间 - Si Prefecture, a place along the Bian River.
  • 浮休集 - *Fuxiu Ji*, a collection of stories or anecdotes, likely a literary work from the Song Dynasty.

[1009]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 干道七年 - The seventh year of the Ganda period (1167-1173 AD) during the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 大饥 - Severe famine.
  • 尤甚 - Especially severe or more severe.
  • 民多馁死 - Many people died of hunger.
  • 遣吏 - To send an official.
  • 往衡山岳市 - To go to the market or trading area near Mount Heng.
  • 买朴硝等物 - To buy items such as mirabilite (芒硝), used in traditional Chinese medicine.
  • 造甲乘马以行 - To make armor and ride a horse to travel.
  • 缘道践人麦田 - To trample on people's wheat fields along the road.
  • 或以米饲马 - Sometimes to feed the horse with rice.
  • 忽天色斗暗 - Suddenly the sky became very dark.
  • 不辨人物 - Unable to distinguish people and things.
  • 雷声大震 - A loud thunderclap.
  • 良久开晴 - For a long time, the sky cleared up.
  • 济与马皆仆地死矣 - Both the official and the horse fell to the ground and died.
  • 邵州以事申转运司 - The prefecture reported the incident to the transport office.
  • 揭榜一路以示戒 - To post a notice along the way to warn.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 干道 - The reign title of Emperor Xiaozong of Song.
  • 邵州 - A prefecture in Hunan Province during the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 蒋济 - A proper name of an official sent by the prefecture.
  • 衡山 - A famous mountain in Hunan Province, known for its scenic beauty and cultural significance.
  • 转运司 - An administrative office responsible for transportation and logistics in the Song Dynasty.
  • 揭榜 - The practice of posting notices or edicts in public places to inform the populace.

Proper Nouns

  • 干道 - The reign title of Emperor Xiaozong of Song.
  • 邵州 - A prefecture in Hunan Province during the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 蒋济 - A proper name of an official sent by the prefecture.
  • 衡山 - A famous mountain in Hunan Province.
  • 转运司 - An administrative office in the Song Dynasty.
  • 陈从古 - A proper name of a transport judge.

[1010]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 皂衣髽妇 - Compound noun meaning 'a woman dressed in black clothes with braided hair,' possibly carrying cultural connotations of mourning or simplicity.
  • 天寒夜长 - Poetic way to describe the winter season, literally 'cold and long night.'
  • 举首 - Gesture of looking up or showing respect, meaning 'to raise one's head.'
  • 放酒走出 - Sudden departure, meaning 'to throw down the wine and walk out.'
  • 悉所见白周公 - To tell Master Zhou about everything I saw, using the first-person humble '悉' (all) and '白' (to inform).
  • 举其腹视之 - To lift its belly and look at it, referring to the action of turning the ancient object over.
  • 唐干封年造 - Made during the Tang dynasty's Gaofeng period (666-674), indicating the age of the object.
  • 即碎之 - To break it immediately, describing the action taken after identifying the object.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 婺源 - A county in modern-day Jiangxi province, historically known for its scholars and cultural significance.
  • 常州宜兴 - A city in modern-day Jiangsu province, known for its ceramics and historical importance.
  • 干道六年 - The sixth year of the Ganda period (1169) under Emperor Xiaozong of Song, a time of cultural and political stability.
  • 周参政 - Refers to Zhou, a high-ranking official in the Song dynasty, likely a '参知政事' (Censor-in-Chief or Vice Premier).
  • 皂衣 - Black clothing, often associated with mourning or simplicity, which may carry symbolic meaning in the story.
  • 髽妇 - A woman with braided hair, possibly indicating a specific social status or role.
  • 银杅 - A silver container or cup, likely used for serving wine.
  • 古铛 - An ancient tripod or vessel, often used for cooking or storing food.
  • 木牛杅 - Wooden containers, possibly used for storing food or other items.

Proper Nouns

  • 汪生 - The name of the protagonist, a scholar from Wuyuan.
  • 周参政 - The name of the high-ranking official, likely a '参知政事' (Censor-in-Chief or Vice Premier).
  • 干道六年 - The year 1169 in the reign of Emperor Xiaozong of Song.
  • 常州宜兴 - The location where the story takes place, a city in modern-day Jiangsu province.
  • 皂衣髽妇 - The name of the mysterious woman, described by her appearance.
  • 银杅 - The name of the silver container or cup used in the story.
  • 古铛 - The name of the ancient tripod or vessel used in the story.

[1011]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 沅州某邑村寺中 - Locative phrase meaning 'in a village temple of a certain county in Yuanzhou.'
  • 僧行者十数辈 - Literally 'ten or so monks,' referring to a group of monks.
  • 寺侧某秀才善妖术 - 'A certain scholar near the temple was skilled in sorcery.'
  • 能制其命 - 'Could control their fate,' meaning the scholar had the power to influence the monks' lives.
  • 凡僧出入 - 'Whenever monks came and went,' referring to the monks' movements.
  • 必往告 - 'He would always inform them,' indicating the scholar's habit of warning the monks.
  • 得贝亲施必中分 - 'If he received offerings from the monks, he would split them evenly,' suggesting a fair distribution.
  • 虽鸡犬亦不可容 - 'Even chickens and dogs were not allowed,' emphasizing the scholar's strict control.
  • 客固不悟也 - 'The guest monk was completely unaware,' highlighting his lack of understanding.
  • 饥甚 - 'He was very hungry,' describing the monk's condition.
  • 入厨取食毕 - 'He went into the kitchen to get food and ate it,' indicating his actions.
  • 自闭三门 - 'He locked the three doors himself,' emphasizing his isolation.
  • 升佛殿坐佛脚下 - 'He climbed up to the Buddha hall and sat at the feet of the Buddha,' describing his position.
  • 以袈裟蒙头 - 'He covered his head with his robe,' indicating his state of preparation.
  • 诵楞严咒 - 'He recited the Lengyan spell,' referring to a specific Buddhist mantra.
  • 夜过半 - 'Midnight had passed,' indicating the time.
  • 迅雷一声起 - 'A sudden clap of thunder sounded,' describing the weather.
  • 霹雳继之 - 'This was followed by a crack of lightning,' emphasizing the intensity of the storm.
  • 而窗棂间月色如昼 - 'And the moonlight through the window panes was as bright as day,' describing the scene.
  • 俄闻铃铎音 - 'Soon, he heard the sound of bells and cymbals,' indicating a supernatural event.
  • 若数壮夫负巨木 - 'As if several strong men were carrying a large piece of wood,' describing the sound metaphorically.
  • 欲上复下 - 'Trying to lift it up and then put it down,' continuing the metaphor.
  • 如是三四反 - 'This happened three or four times,' indicating repetition.
  • 又若失脚而堕 - 'And then, as if they had lost their footing and fell,' describing a sudden change.
  • 遂悄无所闻 - 'Then there was silence,' indicating the end of the event.
  • 天明出视 - 'At dawn, he went out to look,' describing his actions.
  • 得四纸人于阶下 - 'He found four paper figures at the bottom of the stairs,' indicating the discovery.
  • 旁一棺亦纸为之 - 'Next to them was a paper coffin,' describing the additional item.
  • 漫折于怀中 - 'He casually folded them and put them in his bosom,' indicating his reaction.
  • 少顷众至 - 'Soon, the other monks arrived,' describing their arrival.
  • 见之惊 - 'They were shocked to see it,' conveying their reaction.
  • 争问夜所睹 - 'They asked him about what he had seen at night,' indicating their curiosity.
  • 具以本末告之 - 'He told them the whole story,' describing his response.
  • 且云、彼人习邪法 - 'And he said, 'That person practiced dark magic,''' indicating his belief.
  • 既不能害人 - 'But he could not harm others,' suggesting the scholar's limitations.
  • 当自被其害 - 'He must have been harmed by it himself,' indicating the scholar's fate.
  • 试共往扣 - 'Let's go together and check,' suggesting a plan of action.
  • 则秀才果已毙 - 'Indeed, the scholar was found dead,' confirming the outcome.
  • 四体如刀裂 - 'His body was as if cut by a knife,' describing the condition of the corpse.
  • 寺以告县 - 'The temple reported this to the county,' indicating the official response.
  • 遣巡检索忠者体究其事云 - 'The county sent someone to investigate and report back,' describing the investigation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 沅州 - Refers to a region in Hunan province during the Southern Song dynasty.
  • 绍兴三十年 - The thirtieth year of the Shaoxing era, corresponding to the year 1160.
  • 客僧 - Refers to a traveling monk, a common figure in Chinese literature.
  • 楞严咒 - A famous Buddhist mantra from the Lengyan Sutra (楞严经), known for its protective and transformative powers.
  • 纸人 - Paper figures, often used in traditional Chinese rituals and believed to have magical properties.

Proper Nouns

  • 紹興 - Era name of the Southern Song; rendered as Shaoxing.

[1012]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 德清樹妖 - Compound noun referring to a tree spirit or tree demon in Deqing, likely a local legend or supernatural entity.
  • 浙西都監 - Refers to a military or administrative official in charge of the western region of Zhejiang province.
  • 行天心法 - Refers to the practice of the 'Heavenly Heart Method,' a form of Daoist exorcism or ritual.
  • 治不效 - Means 'the treatment was ineffective' or 'the ritual did not work.'
  • 鬼挫辱 - Means 'the ghost humiliated' or 'the spirit insulted,' indicating that the ghost not only caused trouble but also showed disrespect.
  • 齋戒七日 - Means 'to fast and abstain for seven days,' a common ritual preparation for spiritual or religious activities.
  • 書符 - Refers to writing magical symbols or talismans, a common practice in Daoist rituals.
  • 禹步 - Refers to a specific ritualistic walking pattern associated with the legendary Chinese emperor Yu the Great, often used in exorcism rituals.
  • 震雷從空起 - Means 'a thunderbolt struck from the sky,' indicating a dramatic and supernatural event.
  • 析為兩 - Means 'split into two,' describing the physical effect of the thunderbolt on the tree.
  • 怪遂掃迹 - Means 'the monster was completely eradicated' or 'the spirit vanished,' indicating the successful outcome of the ritual.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 宋安国 - The name of the protagonist, a military or administrative official.
  • 浙西 - Refers to the western region of Zhejiang province, indicating the geographical setting of the story.
  • 德清 - A place name in Zhejiang province, where the story takes place.
  • 天心法 - A specific Daoist ritual or exorcism technique, indicating the religious and cultural context of the story.
  • 禹步 - A ritualistic walking pattern associated with Daoist exorcism, indicating the use of traditional practices.
  • 震雷 - A supernatural event, often associated with divine intervention or powerful spiritual forces.

Proper Nouns

  • 宋安国 - The name of the protagonist.
  • 德清 - A place name in Zhejiang province.
  • 浙西 - A geographical region in Zhejiang province.
  • 天心法 - A specific Daoist ritual or exorcism technique.
  • 禹步 - A ritualistic walking pattern associated with Daoist exorcism.

[1013]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 郭签判 - A title referring to a signatory official, likely a local government official in charge of a county or district.
  • 菆女柩 - A term referring to the coffin of a deceased woman, with '菆' being an archaic term for 'coffin.'
  • 大为妖害 - A phrase meaning 'greatly causing harm or mischief,' often used to describe supernatural disturbances.
  • 徙葬 - A term meaning 'relocating the burial,' indicating a change in the burial site.
  • 扃此屋三间 - A phrase meaning 'locking this three-room house,' with '扃' being an archaic term for 'locking.'
  • 侍卫步军 - Refers to the imperial guard or infantry, specifically the foot soldiers of the imperial guard.
  • 遣将卒 - Means 'sending soldiers,' indicating the dispatch of military personnel.
  • 近郊牧马 - A phrase meaning 'herding horses in the outskirts,' referring to the practice of keeping horses in the outskirts of a city.
  • 宗室子赵大 - Refers to a member of the imperial family named Zhao Da, with '宗室' indicating a member of the imperial clan.
  • 诣寺假屋沽酒 - A phrase meaning 'visiting the temple to borrow a room for buying wine,' with '诣' meaning 'to visit' and '沽酒' meaning 'buying wine.'
  • 无闲舍 - A phrase meaning 'no vacant room,' indicating the absence of available space.
  • 以鬼故 - A phrase meaning 'because of the ghost,' indicating the reason for the room's unavailability.
  • 不为人所欲 - A phrase meaning 'not desired by people,' indicating that the room is avoided due to its association with a ghost.
  • 非所以处君也 - A phrase meaning 'not suitable for your residence,' indicating that the room is not appropriate for the visitor's stay.
  • 即日启门 - A phrase meaning 'opening the door on the same day,' indicating the immediate action taken by the visitor.
  • 通三室为一 - A phrase meaning 'connecting the three rooms into one,' indicating the modification of the room's layout.
  • 正中设榻 - A phrase meaning 'setting up a bed in the center,' indicating the arrangement of the room.
  • 枕剑而卧 - A phrase meaning 'sleeping with a sword as a pillow,' indicating a defensive posture.
  • 夜漏方上 - A phrase meaning 'the night watch was just starting,' indicating the early part of the night.
  • 飒然出 - A phrase meaning 'suddenly appeared,' indicating the sudden emergence of the ghost.
  • 艳籹鲜服 - A phrase meaning 'wearing beautiful and fresh clothes,' indicating the ghost's appearance.
  • 立于前 - A phrase meaning 'standing in front,' indicating the ghost's position relative to the visitor.
  • 问之再三 - A phrase meaning 'asking repeatedly,' indicating the visitor's persistence in questioning the ghost.
  • 皆不对 - A phrase meaning 'all without response,' indicating the ghost's silence.
  • 遽起抱之 - A phrase meaning 'quickly got up and embraced it,' indicating the visitor's sudden action.
  • 颇窘畏 - A phrase meaning 'very embarrassed and fearful,' indicating the ghost's reaction to the embrace.
  • 为欲去之状 - A phrase meaning 'as if trying to leave,' indicating the ghost's attempt to escape.
  • 俄顷间如烟雾而散 - A phrase meaning 'in a moment, like smoke and mist, it dispersed,' indicating the ghost's disappearance.
  • 怀中了无物 - A phrase meaning 'nothing in his arms,' indicating the absence of any tangible object.
  • 自是帖然 - A phrase meaning 'since then, it was peaceful,' indicating the cessation of the ghost's disturbances.
  • 赵居之十余年 - A phrase meaning 'Zhao lived there for over ten years,' indicating the duration of the visitor's stay.
  • 不复有所睹 - A phrase meaning 'no longer saw anything,' indicating the absence of further supernatural occurrences.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 湖州德清县宝觉寺 - Refers to the Baoguo Temple in Deqing County, Huzhou, a specific location in Zhejiang Province, China.
  • 签判 - A title referring to a local government official, likely a county or district-level official.
  • 侍卫步军 - Refers to the imperial guard or infantry, specifically the foot soldiers of the imperial guard.
  • 宗室子 - Refers to a member of the imperial family, indicating a high social status.
  • 沽酒 - Refers to the practice of buying wine, a common social activity in ancient China.
  • 夜漏 - Refers to the night watch, indicating the time of night.
  • 宗室 - Refers to the imperial clan, indicating a member of the royal family.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '郭签判女' is ambiguous. It could mean 'the daughter of a signatory official' or 'a woman who was a signatory official.' The context suggests the former.
  • The phrase '菆女柩于僧房' is a bit elliptical. It means 'the coffin of the deceased woman was placed in the temple room,' but the elliptical structure requires careful translation.
  • The phrase '大为妖害' is a bit ambiguous. It could mean 'greatly causing harm' or 'greatly causing mischief.' The context suggests the former.
  • The phrase '徙葬而物怪如初' is elliptical. It means 'after relocating the burial, the supernatural disturbances continued as before,' but the elliptical structure requires careful translation.
  • The phrase '寺中扃此屋三间' is elliptical. It means 'the temple locked this three-room house,' but the elliptical structure requires careful translation.
  • The phrase '不敢居' is a bit ambiguous. It could mean 'dared not live' or 'dared not stay.' The context suggests the latter.
  • The phrase '侍卫步军遣将卒来近郊牧马' is a bit elliptical. It means 'the imperial guard sent soldiers to the outskirts to herd horses,' but the elliptical structure requires careful translation.
  • The phrase '宗室子赵大诣寺假屋沽酒' is a bit elliptical. It means 'the member of the imperial family, Zhao Da, visited the temple to borrow a room for buying wine,' but the elliptical structure requires careful translation.
  • The phrase '僧云、无闲舍' is a bit elliptical. It means 'the monk said, 'There is no vacant room,' but the elliptical structure requires careful translation.
  • The phrase '独彼三间，以鬼故，不为人所欲' is a bit elliptical. It means 'only those three rooms, because of the ghost, were not desired by people,' but the elliptical structure requires careful translation.
  • The phrase '然非所以处君也' is a bit elliptical. It means 'but it is not suitable for your residence,' but the elliptical structure requires careful translation.
  • The phrase '即日启门' is a bit elliptical. It means 'opening the door on the same day,' but the elliptical structure requires careful translation.
  • The phrase '通三室为一' is a bit elliptical. It means 'connecting the three rooms into one,' but the elliptical structure requires careful translation.
  • The phrase '正中设榻' is a bit elliptical. It means 'setting up a bed in the center,' but the elliptical structure requires careful translation.
  • The phrase '枕剑而卧' is a bit elliptical. It means 'sleeping with a sword as a pillow,' but the elliptical structure requires careful translation.
  • The phrase '夜漏方上' is a bit elliptical. It means 'the night watch was just starting,' but the elliptical structure requires careful translation.
  • The phrase '飒然出' is a bit elliptical. It means 'suddenly appeared,' but the elliptical structure requires careful translation.
  • The phrase '艳籹鲜服' is a bit elliptical. It means 'wearing beautiful and fresh clothes,' but the elliptical structure requires careful translation.
  • The phrase '立于前' is a bit elliptical. It means 'standing in front,' but the elliptical structure requires careful translation.
  • The phrase '问之再三' is a bit elliptical. It means 'asking repeatedly,' but the elliptical structure requires careful translation.

[1014]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 以酒库摧陋 - Means 'because the wine cellar was dilapidated,' indicating the reason for the renovation.
  • 买民屋数区 - Means 'bought several houses from the people,' referring to the purchase of private residences to expand the wine cellar.
  • 即其处撤而新之 - Means 'demolished and rebuilt them there,' describing the process of renovation.
  • 精治案魑魅 - Means 'expert in dealing with ghosts and demons,' referring to Mr. Wang's specialized skills in exorcism.
  • 不假符水咒祓 - Means 'without needing talismanic water or incantations,' indicating that Mr. Wang's abilities are natural and do not rely on external rituals.
  • 默睹 - Means 'to see silently,' suggesting that Mr. Wang can perceive supernatural entities without verbal or visible cues.
  • 大蛇枉死 - Means 'a snake died unjustly,' referring to the mysterious death of a snake.
  • 左右闻者毛悚 - Means 'those who heard were terrified,' indicating the reaction of the people present.
  • 蛇影大如椽 - Means 'the snake's shadow was as large as a beam,' describing the size of the snake's shadow.
  • 袤丈 - Means 'ten feet long,' providing a specific measurement for the snake's length.
  • 具询主吏 - Means 'carefully questioned the officials,' referring to the investigation into the snake's death.
  • 不能记蛇事云 - Means 'but could not remember the snake's story,' indicating that the officials were unaware of the snake's death.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 欧阳 - Refers to Ouyang Xiu, a prominent figure in Chinese history, likely the author or a key figure in the story.
  • 镇江 - Refers to Zhenjiang, a city in China, providing the geographical setting.
  • 总领所酒官 - Refers to the official responsible for the wine cellar, indicating the bureaucratic context.
  • 王先生 - Refers to Mr. Wang, a specialist in dealing with ghosts and demons, adding a supernatural element to the story.
  • 长沙 - Refers to Changsha, a city in China, providing the origin of Mr. Wang.
  • 魑魅 - Refers to ghosts and demons, a common theme in Chinese folklore and literature.
  • 符水咒祓 - Refers to talismanic water and incantations, traditional methods of exorcism in Chinese culture.
  • 酒匠 - Refers to a wine maker, indicating the occupation of the person who died by fire.
  • 库典 - Refers to a warehouse supervisor, indicating the occupation of the person who committed suicide.
  • 右三事 - Refers to the three events described, emphasizing the significance of the story.

Proper Nouns

  • 欧阳 - Ouyang Xiu, a prominent figure in Chinese history.
  • 镇江 - Zhenjiang, a city in China.
  • 王先生 - Mr. Wang, a specialist in dealing with ghosts and demons.
  • 长沙 - Changsha, a city in China.
  • 酒匠 - A wine maker.
  • 库典 - A warehouse supervisor.
  • 右三事 - The three events described in the story.

[1015]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 年九十而终 - Literally means 'to die at the age of ninety,' a common phrase used to describe a long life.
  • 六月降霜 - An unusual weather event, often used metaphorically to describe an unexpected or abnormal situation.
  • 芦沉石浮 - Literally means 'reeds sink and stones float,' a paradoxical statement that suggests an inversion of natural order, often used to describe an extraordinary or impossible situation.
  • 间隔寒泉 - Literally means 'the cold spring is separated,' which could be interpreted as a metaphor for isolation or separation from something essential.
  • 高山一丘 - Literally means 'a high mountain is just a small hill,' a paradoxical statement that suggests a reduction in scale or importance, often used to describe a situation where something significant is diminished.
  • 公直为上 - Literally means 'uprightness is the highest virtue,' emphasizing the importance of integrity and honesty.
  • 勿攘田土钱物 - Literally means 'do not steal land, money, or goods,' a moral instruction against theft and dishonesty.
  • 见专治此等事 - Literally means 'seeing and dealing with such matters,' referring to the responsibility of handling or addressing certain issues.
  • 更有数语 - Literally means 'there are several more words,' indicating that there are additional phrases or statements.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 处州胡教授母 - Refers to the mother of Professor Hu from Chuzhou, a place in Zhejiang Province. The term '教授' (professor) indicates a respected academic or scholar.
  • 冥司 - Refers to the underworld or the realm of the dead, a common concept in Chinese mythology and folklore.
  • 武翼郎 - A military rank in the Song Dynasty, indicating a high-ranking military official.
  • 建昌新城 - Refers to a place in Jiangxi Province, indicating the location of the wealthy household.
  • 绍兴三十二年 - Refers to the year 1162 in the Southern Song Dynasty, using the era name 'Shaoxing.'
  • 黄通理 - The name of a doctor, indicating a medical professional.
  • 汤法先 - The name of a shaman, indicating a spiritual or religious practitioner.
  • 僧志通 - The name of a monk, indicating a Buddhist practitioner.

Proper Nouns

  • 处州 - A place name, referring to Chuzhou in Zhejiang Province.

[1019]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 則不堪說 - Elliptical phrase likely meaning 'then it is too unbearable to speak of' or 'too painful to describe.'
  • 既無屋可居 - Straightforward expression meaning 'since there is no house to live in.'
  • 多伏於屠肆肉案下 - Literally 'often crouch under the meat tables in butchery shops.' Implies desperation and poverty.
  • 往往為犬所驚逐 - 'Often frightened and chased by dogs.' Suggests vulnerability and exposure.
  • 良以為苦 - 'He finds it very painful.' '良' is used for emphasis, similar to 'indeed' or 'very.'
  • 而無可奈何 - 'But there is nothing to be done.' Common expression of helplessness.
  • 史與錢兩千遣去 - 'Shi gave two thousand coins to send them away.' '遣去' is a common Classical Chinese expression for sending someone away.
  • 明日、復以四鴨至 - 'The next day, he came again with four ducks.' '復' indicates repetition or recurrence.
  • 自是時時一來 - 'From then on, he would come at times.' Implies irregular but recurring visits.
  • 吾人也。而日與鬼語。吾其不久於世乎 - 'I am a human, yet I speak daily with ghosts. Am I not to live long on this earth?' Rhetorical expression of despair and existential doubt.
  • 立已知之 - 'Lì already knew this.' Concise way of expressing that someone has already understood or foreseen something.
  • 公無用疑我 - 'You need not doubt me.' Direct and assertive statement to dispel suspicion.
  • 獨不見公家大養娘乎 - 'Have you not seen your family's elderly servant?' '大養娘' is likely a term of respect for an elderly woman, possibly a nurse or caretaker.
  • 袖出白石兩小顆 - 'He took out two small white stones from his sleeve.' Act of producing something from the sleeve is common in Classical Chinese texts to emphasize a magical or supernatural element.
  • 乞以淬火中 - 'Please put them into the fire.' '乞' is a polite request, '淬火' is a metallurgical term meaning 'quenching in fire.'
  • 當知立言不妄 - 'You will know that my words are not empty.' Affirms the speaker’s credibility or prophetic insight.
  • 此媪蓋史長子乳母 - 'This old woman was the nurse of Shi's eldest son.' '蓋' is a particle used for clarification or explanation.
  • 居家三十年矣 - 'She has lived in the household for thirty years.' Indicates long-term service and familiarity with the family.
  • 史入戲之曰 - 'Shi entered into the jest and said.' '戲之' implies a playful or mocking tone, possibly a form of teasing or testing.
  • 外人說汝是鬼如何 - 'What would you say if outsiders claimed you are a ghost?' Rhetorical question used to provoke a response or test the woman’s reaction.
  • 真合作鬼 - 'Truly, you are fit to be a ghost.' Sarcastic or mocking remark implying ghostly appearance or demeanor.
  • 雖極忿愠 - 'Even though she was extremely angry.' '忿愠' is an expression of anger or resentment.
  • 而了無惧容 - 'Yet she showed no sign of fear.' Contrast between internal anger and external composure.
  • 適小妾熨帛在旁 - 'Just then, the concubine was ironing clothes nearby.' Provides context for the scene, showing the woman was not alone.
  • 史試投石於斗中 - 'Shi tested by throwing the stone into the cauldron.' '試' means 'to test' or 'to try out.'
  • 少頃焰起 - 'After a short while, the flame arose.' Describes the immediate effect of the stone in the fire.
  • 媪顏色即索然 - 'The old woman's expression immediately became dull.' '索然' means 'lifeless' or 'expressionless.'
  • 漸益淡淺 - 'Gradually becoming more pale and faint.' Suggests a fading or dissolving effect, possibly of the spirit.
  • 如水墨中影 - 'Like a shadow in ink.' Metaphor for something faint, indistinct, or ephemeral.
  • 忽寂無所見 - 'Suddenly, it was silent and nothing was seen.' Marks the disappearance or vanishing of the spirit or image.
  • 王立亦不復來 - 'Wang Li did not come again.' Indicates the ghostly presence has left or disappeared.
  • 予於丙志載李吉事 - 'I have already recorded the story of Li Ji in the *Bingzhi*.' '丙志' is likely a reference to a historical or anecdotal record.
  • 固已笑鬼技之相似 - 'I have already laughed at how similar the ghost's trick was.' Statement of comparison and amusement.
  • 此又稍異云 - 'This one is slightly different, however.' Concluding remark indicating a minor variation from the previously mentioned case.
  • 朱椿年說。聞之於史倅 - 'This was said by Zhu Chunya, who heard it from Shi's deputy.' Provides the source of the narrative, indicating it is a second-hand account.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 史 - Likely a surname, possibly referring to a historical figure or a character in the narrative. May be a generic name for a scholar or official.
  • 王立 - Possibly a name of the ghost or supernatural figure, though not a known historical figure. May be a fictional or local name used in the story.
  • 大養娘 - Term referring to an elderly woman, possibly a nurse, servant, or caretaker. Used in certain regional or familial contexts.
  • 乳母 - A nurse or wet nurse, specifically one who breastfed a child. Indicates the woman’s long-standing role in the household.
  • 史倅 - 'Shi’s deputy' or 'Shi’s assistant,' likely referring to an official or subordinate of Shi.
  • 朱椿年 - Name of a person, possibly a historian or storyteller, cited as the source of the account.
  • 丙志 - Likely a reference to a historical or anecdotal record, possibly a work of literature or chronicle. '丙' is a heavenly stem used in the Chinese sexagenary cycle.
  • 李吉 - Name mentioned in the *Bingzhi*, likely a figure from a previous story or historical record.
  • 鬼技 - 'Ghost technique' or 'ghostly trick,' used to describe supernatural or magical actions, often with a humorous or ironic tone.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 靈泉鬼魋 - A descriptive title combining the name of a location (灵泉寺, Lingquan Temple) with a term (鬼魋, ghostly thorn) that suggests something eerie or supernatural.

  • 居民目为鬼魋 - 'The residents regard it as a ghostly thorn,' where 目为 (mù wéi) means to consider or regard as.

  • 才施数斧 - 'Only chopped a few strokes,' 斩 (zhǎn) means to chop.
  • 木中血流 - 'Blood flowed from the trees,' 描述了砍树时出现的异常现象。
  • 仆惧乃止 - 'The servants were afraid and stopped,' 惧 (jù) means to be afraid.
  • 还白焉 - 'Returned and reported it,' 白 (bái) means to report or inform.
  • 王挞其为首者二人 - 'The king beat the two leaders,' 为首者 (wéi shǒu zhě) means the leaders or chief.
  • 只是老树皮汁出 - 'It was just the sap of the old tree,' 汁出 (zhī chū) means the sap or juice coming out.
  • 安得血 - 'How could it be blood?' 安 (ān) is a rhetorical particle meaning how or how could.
  • 群仆知不可免 - 'The group of servants knew they could not avoid it,' 不可免 (bù kě miǎn) means unavoidable.
  • 共买纸钱焚之 - 'Bought paper money to burn it,' 纸钱 (zhǐ qián) is a type of offering paper used in rituals.
  • 被发斫树 - 'Cut the trees with disheveled hair,' 被发 (bèi fà) means to have disheveled hair, indicating a state of distress.
  • 每下一斧 - 'Every time they chopped with an axe,' 下 (xià) means to lower or use.
  • 即呼曰 - 'Immediately called out,' 即 (jí) means immediately.
  • 王抚干使我斫 - 'King Fugan made me chop,' 抚干 (fú gān) is the name of the king.
  • 竟空其林 - 'Finally cleared the forest,' 竟 (jìng) means finally or completely.
  • 得薪三千束 - 'Obtained three thousand bundles of firewood,' 束 (shù) is a unit of measurement.
  • 时绍兴十三年也 - 'It was the thirteenth year of the Shaoxing era,' 绍兴 (Shào Xīng) is the era name of Emperor Gaozong of Song.
  • 经月 - 'After a month,' 经 (jīng) means after or through.
  • 王疽发于背 - 'The king developed a boil on his back,' 疽 (jū) is a boil or abscess.
  • 自言见祟物 - 'Said he saw a supernatural object,' 见 (jiàn) means to see, and 祟物 (suì wù) means a supernatural object.
  • 既死 - 'After he died,' 既 (jì) means already or after.
  • 祟犹不去 - 'The supernatural still did not leave,' 去 (qù) means to leave.
  • 众为别栽木其处以谢之 - 'The people planted trees in another place to appease it,' 别 (bié) means another, and 谢 (xiè) means to appease or thank.
  • 今蔚然成林 - 'Now it has grown into a lush forest,' 蔚然 (wèi rán) means lush or flourishing.
  • 祟始息 - 'The supernatural finally ceased,' 息 (xī) means to cease or stop.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '和州一老妇人携两男' introduces the main characters: an elderly woman from Hezhou with two sons.
  • The phrase '大者二十六岁，小者二十岁' specifies the ages of the two sons, but the order of '大者' and '小者' can be ambiguous without context.
  • The phrase '云在孕皆二十四月乃生' is hyperbolic and requires careful translation to convey the unusual nature of their conception and birth.
  • The long descriptive phrase '遍体长黑毛有光采，眼睛如点漆，白处如碧云，唇朱如丹' must be read as a single unit, describing the physical appearance of the individuals.
  • The phrase '皆善相术' indicates that both individuals were skilled in physiognomy, which is a key detail in the story.
  • The sequence of actions '召赴京师，婴金帛，遣归州' must be read as a single unit, describing the treatment they received.
  • The phrase '通判黄达如邀问相' introduces the vice-governor Huang Daru and his interest in their fortunes.
  • The prediction '可至大夫与州，生六子，其半得官' must be read as a single unit, describing the future of the eldest son.
  • The colloquial expression '将钱来，数至四十四钱' must be read as a single unit, indicating the age of the eldest son.
  • The sequence of dialogue '是么，弟曰，是' must be read as a single unit, confirming the age.
  • The phrase '相长女，问有封邑否' introduces the prediction for the eldest daughter.
  • The phrase '问寿得五十三钱' indicates the predicted lifespan of the eldest daughter.
  • The phrase '相次女得二十七钱' indicates the predicted lifespan of the second daughter.
  • The phrase '凡阅数人，率如是而已' summarizes the predictions for several individuals, indicating a consistent pattern.
  • The phrase '是后二十余年' sets the time frame for the subsequent events.
  • The sequence of events '黄仕历御史郎，官至朝请大夫，知徽州而卒' must be read as a single unit, describing the career of Huang Daru.
  • The phrase '长女享年，如所得钱之数' indicates the actual lifespan of the eldest daughter, matching the predicted number.
  • The sequence of events '次女以绍兴甲子岁，从其夫祝生赴衡山尉，溺死于江' must be read as a single unit, describing the fate of the second daughter.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 谒蜀人韩慥问命 - Refers to the act of consulting someone about one's fate, a common practice in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 必作官人 - Indicates that the person will definitely become an official, reflecting the value placed on social status and power in traditional Chinese society.
  • 不读书亦可 - Suggests that becoming an official is possible even without studying, a controversial idea in traditional Chinese society.
  • 俟过二十二日立秋 - Indicates that the person should wait until the twenty-second day after the beginning of autumn, an important seasonal marker in Chinese culture.
  • 别相访 - The practice of visiting someone for a second consultation was common in traditional Chinese divination and fortune-telling.
  • 当细为君说 - The idea of providing a detailed explanation was important in traditional Chinese divination, where precision and thoroughness were valued.
  • 叶大怒 - The strong emotional reaction of the person to the prediction reflects the high stakes and emotional investment in traditional Chinese divination.
  • 几欲棰辱之 - The idea of physical punishment for a predictor was a serious threat in traditional Chinese society, reflecting the power dynamics between the client and the predictor.
  • 同坐黄德琬劝使去 - The role of a mediator or advisor in traditional Chinese divination was important, as it helped to manage the emotional and social dynamics of the consultation.
  • 后十六日叶得病 - The timing of the illness is significant, as it aligns with the predictor's warning about the beginning of autumn.
  • 即呕血 - Vomiting blood was a serious symptom in traditional Chinese medicine, often associated with a critical condition.
  • 始以为忧 - The initial concern about the situation reflects the uncertainty and anxiety associated with traditional Chinese divination.
  • 叶不孝不义 - Describes the person's unfilial and unrighteous behavior, reflecting the moral values of traditional Chinese society.
  • 鬼神当殛之 - Suggests that the spirits should punish the person, reflecting the belief in supernatural justice in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 客死非不幸也 - Indicates that dying as a stranger is not unfortunate, reflecting a different perspective on death and fate in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 韩之术一何神哉 - Praises the predictor's skill as wonderful, reflecting the high regard for divination and fortune-telling in traditional Chinese society.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 茅山道人 - Literally 'Taoist recluse of Mount Mao,' referring to a Taoist practitioner or hermit associated with Mount Mao, a famous Taoist center in Jiangsu.

  • 不甚詳的 - Not very detailed or not very clearly recorded.
  • 復載于此 - Is recorded again here or is recounted here.
  • 绍兴癸酉三月 - In the third month of the year *Gui You* during the *Shaoxing* era, a Chinese calendar date where *Shaoxing* is the reign title of Emperor Gaozong of Song (1131–1162).
  • 诣茅山观鹤会 - Visited Mount Mao to attend a gathering of cranes, a Daoist practice as cranes are symbolic of immortality and spiritual practice.
  • 访刘蓑衣于黑虎洞林间 - Visited Liu Suoyi in the forest near Heihutong (Black Tiger Cave). *蓑衣* is a type of raincoat made of straw or reeds, often associated with hermits or Daoist practitioners.
  • 席地饮酒 - Sat on the ground and drank wine, a common practice in informal gatherings.
  • 遣小史呼能唱词道人 - Sent a young attendant to summon a Daoist who could sing poetry or lyrics. *小史* is a term for a young attendant or servant.
  • 俄二十辈来 - Soon, twenty came. *辈* is a classifier for people, often used in lists or groups.
  • 迨夜步月行歌 - By night, they walked, moonlight in their path, singing songs, a poetic description of a nighttime gathering.
  • 至清真观路口道堂 - Arrived at the entrance of the *Qingzhen* Temple’s *Dao Tang* (Daoist Hall). *清真* is a term that can mean 'pure and true' or 'Islamic' in later contexts, but here it is likely a Daoist name or title.
  • 各呈其伎 - Each displayed their skill or talent. *伎* refers to performance or artistry.
  • 忽空中如人歌四句 - Suddenly, from the air, a person sang four lines, a supernatural or mystical event.
  • 黄尉能记其二云 - The magistrate Huang could remember two of them. *尉* is a magistrate or local official.
  • 四十三。四十三。一轮明月落清潭 - Forty-three. Forty-three. A full moon falls into the clear pool, a poetic or cryptic statement.
  • 秦正四十三岁矣 - Qin was exactly forty-three years old. *矣* is a particle indicating a completed state.
  • 历扣二十人 - He questioned all twenty people. *扣* can mean to interrogate or question.
  • 此谁所言 - Who said this?
  • 皆曰 - All said.
  • 元未尝发口 - None of them had spoken. *元* is an archaic form of 'none' or 'not.'
  • 乃罢酒而还 - They then stopped drinking and returned.
  • 果卒 - Indeed died. *卒* means 'died.'
  • 前一年 - The year before.
  • 达真黄元道 - The accomplished Daoist Huang Yuandao. *达真* is a title or honorific meaning 'attained truth' or 'true attainment.'
  • 君有冤对 - You have a grudge or injustice against you. *冤对* refers to a grievance or wrong.
  • 切忌四三 - You must avoid forty-three. *切忌* is a strong warning or prohibition.
  • 豤求解释之术 - Qin earnestly sought a method of explanation. *豤* is likely a misspelling or variant of *恳* (earnestly).
  • 时幼儿弄磁瓢为戏 - At that time, a child was playing with a magnetic ladle. *磁瓢* may be a term for a magical or mystical object, possibly a type of ladle or vessel used in Daoist rituals.
  • 黄取其一 - Huang took one of them.
  • 呵祝以授秦 - He chanted a prayer and gave it to Qin. *呵祝* is a ritual act of invoking or blessing.
  • 秦接之手内如火 - When Qin received it, his hand felt as if on fire.
  • 不觉扑于地 - He could not help but fall to the ground.

  • 红丸则可服 - If you get a red pill, you may take it.
  • 后三年 - Three years later.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 法当死 - Refers to the legal system in place during the Song dynasty, which could impose the death penalty for crimes such as treason or espionage.
  • 赏甚重 - The Song dynasty often used rewards to encourage informants, especially during times of war or political instability.

  • 河中府 - A historical administrative division in northern China, likely near the Yellow River.
  • 河滩 - A riverbank or riverside area, common locations for temporary rest or travel stops in ancient times.
  • 一担仆 - A servant carrying a bundle, indicating that the individuals were traveling with attendants and luggage, suggesting some level of social status or preparation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 夷坚丁志 is a collection of supernatural stories compiled by Zhang Yingyu during the Southern Song dynasty.
  • 茅山 is a famous Daoist mountain in Jiangsu province, known for its spiritual practices and immortals.
  • 鹤会 is a gathering of immortals or Daoists, often associated with spiritual cultivation and longevity.
  • 壬午 refers to the year 1159 CE, a specific time period in the Southern Song dynasty.
  • 丹沙泓 is a place name, likely referring to a specific location in the mountain where a hermitage is located.
  • 大茅君 is a prominent figure in Daoist mythology, often associated with Mount Mao.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 田道人者、河北人。避乱南度。居京口。 introduces Tian as a person from Hebei who fled south to avoid chaos and settled in Jingkou (modern-day Zhenjiang).
  • 每岁三月。茅山鹤会。欲与其徒偕往。必有故而辍。 describes Tian's annual intention to attend the gathering at Mount Mao but being repeatedly stopped by some reason.
  • 绝兴壬午之春。始获一游。因留连月余。将归。足疾骤作。不可行。既止即愈。欲行复作。如是者屡矣。 describes Tian's journey to Mount Mao in 1159, his stay for over a month, and his recurring foot pain.
  • 意其缘在此山。祷于神。乞为终焉之计。自尔不复病。 explains Tian's belief that the cause of his foot pain was related to the mountain and his prayer to the gods for a permanent solution.
  • 梦神告曰、此非汝居也。汝自有庵在山中。其址东向者是。宜亟访之。 describes the god's message in Tian's dream, indicating that he has a hermitage in the mountain.
  • 固以为想念所兆。未深信。越数夕。梦如初。犹未决。 describes Tian's initial disbelief and subsequent repeated dreams.
  • 又念身赤立于此。纵得其基。虽草庐岂易能办。是夕梦神怒曰、旬日不迁。必死兹地矣。 describes Tian's fear of standing naked in the mountain and the god's warning in a dream.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 干道三年 - A year in the Gandao era of the Song Dynasty. The Gandao era was the fourth reign title of Emperor Xiaozong of Song (r. 1163–1189). This is a standard historical dating method in Classical Chinese.
  • 濒江之民 - People living near the river. 濒 means 'beside' or 'near,' and 江 is a general term for 'river' or 'great river.'
  • 挈母妻并二子 - Took his mother, wife, and two children. 挈 means 'to carry' or 'to take along.'
  • 难以偕生 - Difficult to live together. 偕生 literally means 'to live together.'
  • 吾家五口 - My family of five. A direct count of people.
  • 减得一口 - To reduce by one person. 减 means 'to reduce,' and 一口 is 'one person.'
  • 遂绝溪而北 - Then crossed the stream and went north. 绝溪 means 'crossed the stream.'
  • 渠必不能渡水 - He will certainly not be able to cross the water. 渠 is a pronoun meaning 'he' or 'that person.'
  • 愍姑老 - Pitied her grandmother's old age. �愍 is a classical term for 'pity' or 'compassion.'
  • 掖之以行 - Supported her while walking. 掖 means 'to support under the arm.'
  • 陷于淖 - Fell into a marsh. �淖 is a swamp or marshy place.
  • 俯而取履 - Bowed down to pick up her shoe. 履 is a shoe.
  • 有石碍其手 - There was a stone that blocked her hand. 碍 means 'to hinder' or 'to block.'
  • 拨去之 - Pushed it aside. 拨去 is a verb phrase.
  • 乃银一笏也 - It was a piece of silver. 笏 is a rectangular tablet used by officials in court, but here it is used metaphorically to mean a 'piece' or 'slab.'
  • 不惟足食 - Not only enough to eat. 不惟 is a classical negation meaning 'not only.'
  • 亦可作小生计 - Also can serve as a small means of livelihood. 生计 is 'means of livelihood.'
  • 却还 - Return back. 却 here is used as a directional particle.
  • 何用去 - Why go away? 何用 is a classical way of saying 'why.'
  • 儿戏沙上 - The children were playing in the sand. 儿戏 is 'children's play.'
  • 为黄黑斑牛衔入林矣 - Was taken into the forest by a yellow-and-black-spotted cow. 衔 is 'to carry' or 'to take into the mouth.'
  • 流血污地 - Blood flowed and stained the ground. 污地 is 'to stain the ground.'
  • 但余骨发存焉 - Only the bones and hair remained. 但 is 'only,' 余 is 'remain,' 存 is 'exist.'
  • 不孝之诛 - The punishment for filial impiety. 诛 is 'punishment' or 'execution.'
  • 其速如此 - Such was its speed. 其 is a demonstrative pronoun, 速 is 'speed,' 如此 is 'like this.'
  • 寓客黄虨彪父自丰城来 - The guest, Huang Qinbiao's father, came from Fengcheng. 寓客 is 'a guest staying in a place,' 父 is 'father.'
  • 财数日事也 - It was only a matter of a few days. 财 is an archaic form of 才, meaning 'only' or 'merely.'

  • 临川 - A county in Jiangxi, also known for its historical and literary significance.

  • 黄虨彪 - Another proper name; likely the son of the guest who brought the story to Blue Shucheng. 誌 is a rare surname, and 彪 is a common character meaning 'fierce' or 'distinguished.'

  • 明日果来。烧通赤火箸。剚入尾闾六七寸。晏然如不觉。继以冷箸涂药。随傅之数反。 - This sequence describes a medical procedure, including burning an iron rod, inserting it, and applying medicine.

  • 财两夕。疮痂尽脱。所烙处肉已平。六窍皆盈实。腹内别生木肠。自是与常人亡异。饮啖倍于他日。所下粪全似鸡。遂娶妻生子。年过五十。疽复发于脐下。洞腹乃死。 - This sequence describes the healing process, the fantastical condition, and the final outcome.
  • 凡无肠而活者四十二年。世间无此病也。二医疑皆异人云。 - This sequence describes the long period of survival, the uniqueness of the condition, and the doctors' conclusion.

[1179]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 田三姑 - A proper name, likely referring to a woman known as 'Tian San Gu' (田三姑), meaning 'Third Aunt Tian' or 'Third Mrs. Tian.' In Chinese naming conventions, '三姑' can be a respectful or affectionate term for an aunt or a woman of a certain age or status.
  • 遣仆至衡山 - 'Sent a servant to Hengshan,' where '遣' means to send or dispatch, and '仆' is a servant or attendant.
  • 招表侄张敏中 - 'To summon her cousin by marriage, Zhang Minzhong.' '表侄' refers to a nephew by marriage, i.e., the son of one's sister-in-law or the daughter of one's brother-in-law.
  • 未克往而田不起 - 'Before he could go, Tian passed away.' '未克往' means 'had not yet been able to go,' and '不起' is a euphemistic way of saying 'died.'
  • 欲托以后事 - 'Wish to entrust her afterlife affairs.' '托以后事' is a classical expression meaning to entrust future matters, often used in the context of death or dying.
  • 欻起坐 - 'Suddenly sat up.' '欻' is an onomatopoeic word meaning 'suddenly' or 'abruptly.'
  • 语言不伦 - 'Spoke incoherently or in a strange manner.' '不伦' means 'not in order' or 'not appropriate.'
  • 咄咄责妹 - 'Chastised her sister harshly.' '咄咄' is an exclamation expressing reproach or accusation.
  • 必欲与我共一婿 - 'Must want to share one husband with me.' This is a supernatural or ghostly statement implying a past relationship or rivalry between the sister and the speaker.
  • 死又不设位祀我 - 'After death, you did not set up a shrine to worship me.' '设位' refers to establishing an ancestral shrine or position, and '祀' is to worship or venerate.
  • 非相率同归不可 - 'We must go together to the afterlife.' '相率' means 'to go together' or 'to follow one another,' and '同归' means 'to go together to the same place,' often used in the context of death or the afterlife.
  • 张谏晓之曰 - 'Zhang tried to reason with her.' '谏' is to advise or remonstrate, and '晓' is to explain or make clear.
  • 此自田叔所为 - 'This is the doing of Tian’s younger brother.' '田叔' refers to Tian’s younger brother, not to be confused with the title 'Tian Shu' or other names.
  • 叔翁万福 - 'May Uncle (or Elder) be well.' '叔翁' is a respectful term for an older male relative or elder, and '万福' is a formulaic expression of good wishes or blessings.
  • 庆孙汝可上床坐 - 'Qing Sun, you may sit on the bed.' '庆孙' is likely the name of a child or family member, and '上床坐' is to sit on the bed.
  • 盖群鬼满室 - 'Indeed, the room was filled with ghosts.' '盖' is a particle used to emphasize or introduce a conclusion, and '群鬼' is 'a group of ghosts.'
  • 左右尽悚 - 'Those around her were all terrified.' '尽悚' means 'all were afraid' or 'all were in fear.'
  • 俄开目变貌 - 'Soon, she opened her eyes and changed appearance.' '俄' means 'soon' or 'in a moment,' and '变貌' is 'changed appearance.'
  • 作田氏音声 - 'Spoke in the voice of Tian’s family.' '作...音声' means 'to make the sound of' or 'to speak in the voice of.'
  • 知县其为姑来 - 'The magistrate knows that it is Aunt (or Lady) coming.' '知县' is the magistrate or county official, and '其为姑来' means 'that it is the aunt coming.'
  • 姑生前有欲言者 - 'Aunt had something to say before her death.' '欲言' is 'wanted to speak' or 'had something to say.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 淄州 - A historical county in Shandong Province, now part of modern-day Zibo.
  • 攸县 - A county in Hunan Province, historically known for its cultural and literary significance.
  • 刘郎中 - 'Liu, the Censorate Official' or 'Liu, the Inspector.' '郎中' was a bureaucratic title in the imperial Chinese system, often referring to an official in the Han or Song dynasties.
  • 衡山 - Hengshan, one of the Five Sacred Mountains of China, located in Hunan Province. It is a site of religious and cultural significance, often associated with Daoist and Buddhist practices.
  • 表侄 - A nephew by marriage, i.e., the son of one’s sister-in-law or the daughter of one’s brother-in-law. This term reflects the complex familial relationships in classical Chinese society.

  • 廖嫂 - 'Liao’s sister-in-law' or 'Liao’s wife’s sister.' In the context of the text, this is the sister of the deceased wife of Tian’s brother.
  • 田三之季父瑴 - 'The youngest uncle of Tian San, Ruo.' '季父' is the youngest brother of one’s father, i.e., the paternal uncle.

  • 唯进独喜食 - 'Only Jin alone likes to eat.' This is a contrast to the family's dietary restrictions.
